summaries.122994: The issue is Android-specific and confirmed not reproducible on iOS. Comments narrow it to Samsung devices (S10+, S20 Ultra, A52S, A54, S21fe) running Android 12–13, with logs showing the IME reporting `servedView != view` when the keyboard is requested at cold-start. Later comments confirm the bug also manifests in release builds but not debug builds, and that a hot-restart or a second run resolves it, suggesting a race condition during app startup before the view is fully served to the IME.
summaries.124789: Comments confirm the bug is reproducible on both stable and master. A commenter adds that even with symmetric padding and `TextAlignVertical.center`, the `OutlineInputBorder` shifts the text a few pixels north compared to `InputBorder.none`, indicating the baseline offset is wrong in all outline-border cases, not just asymmetric ones. A workaround of manually adjusting `height` (line-height) was noted but causes an oversized cursor. The issue was linked to #86093 as related.
summaries.131547: Comments confirm the scroll-jump is still reproducible on Linux when clearing selection via `userUpdateTextEditingValue(TextEditingValue.empty, ...)`. The root cause is that `_scheduleShowCaretOnScreen` fires even when the resulting selection is empty/invalid. A contributor (vongrad) agreed to open a PR but never did, and was eventually unassigned. The issue remained open after re-verification that the bug still occurs on master.
summaries.134092: Comments identify the root cause: Safari/WebKit reports an `TextEditingDeltaInsertion` for a dead-key (e.g., `~`) with an uncollapsed selection, then a separate `TextEditingDeltaNonTextUpdate` to collapse it; Flutter's delta system calls `setEditingState` between these two calls because the editor's selection differs from the IME's, which prevents the follow-up non-text delta from arriving and breaks IME composition. Affects web/Safari only. Renzo-Olivares was assigned to investigate a fix (batching the two deltas), but was later unassigned due to inactivity. Status: stalled.
summaries.167374: Comments confirm the layout jump is Android-only (not iOS) and reproducible on stable (3.29.x) and master. The issue predates 3.27.4, so it is not a recent regression. Investigation with the performance overlay on a Pixel 7 revealed severe ongoing jank even with the keyboard stationary, while other devices (Poco M3) showed normal frame rates, suggesting a device-specific rendering interaction when orientation-change data arrives after the keyboard insets are applied.
summaries.144463: Comments identify this as likely a regression: the original look-up feature author confirmed it worked correctly at merge time, implying a subsequent change broke re-focus. The root cause is linked to app-lifecycle focus resets introduced in #142930, partially reverted via #148612, but the text field still loses focus on master. A connection to shake-to-undo (#150522) was noted, as the native text field keeps the input connection open after losing focus to receive undo commands. PR #173846 has a related comment. Status: assigned (okorohelijah), PR activity noted but issue still open.
summaries.52955: Comments confirm the bug persists through stable 3.7.x and master. The issue affects pasting: `TextCapitalization` is only applied to IME-typed input, not to text set programmatically or pasted. No fix or PR is mentioned in comments; the discussion is reproduction confirmation only.
summaries.90345: Comments confirm the bug reproduces on stable 3.29.3 and master 3.32.0. The root cause is that calling `enterText` on a disabled `TextFormField` leaves the text input connection in an inconsistent state, so re-enabling the field and calling `enterText` again does not update the field value. No fix or PR is mentioned; status is open with triage confirmation.
summaries.96022: Comments confirm the assertion fails on both stable and master when simulating shift-key combinations (e.g., `!`, `*`, `(`) on macOS in tests. The root cause is that `KeyEventSimulator` has no macOS physical-key mapping for logical keys that require Shift; the workaround is to specify `physicalKey` explicitly (e.g., `PhysicalKeyboardKey.digit1`) or simulate Shift+key separately. A broader fix tracking logical-key remapping for shifted keys was tracked in #100456. Status: open; the workaround is documented in comments.
summaries.140739: Comments confirm the cursor-position bug is Windows-specific (not web or macOS) and Korean-specific (Japanese and Chinese do not exhibit it), first appearing around 3.16. The Korean IME builds characters in-place rather than buffering like CJK IMEs, causing the composing cursor to be drawn one position ahead. Commenters link it to a previous related issue (#121376). No PR exists; the issue has been open for over two years with multiple commercial users affected and no workaround. Status: stalled.
summaries.146540: Comments identify the root cause: the fix for #71679 (engine PR #46037) added `InputType.TYPE_TEXT_VARIATION_VISIBLE_PASSWORD` alongside `TYPE_TEXT_FLAG_NO_SUGGESTIONS` to suppress suggestions on Samsung/GBoard devices, but `VISIBLE_PASSWORD` disables emoji input and Chinese IME input. The team confirmed that no Android `InputType` flag combination simultaneously disables suggestions and preserves emoji/CJK input even in native apps, making a clean fix non-trivial. Status: open, waiting for a flag combination solution from the community.
summaries.166462: Comments confirm the bug reproduces on stable and master across all platforms. Root cause analysis from the team: when selecting backwards from a `TextSpan` into a container holding a `WidgetSpan`, `MultiSelectableSelectionContainerDelegate._initSelection` starts searching from the first selectable in the new container rather than the last, breaking backwards traversal while forward selection works correctly. A fix would require `_initSelection` and `_adjustSelection` to detect backwards movement and start from the last selectable. No PR exists; status is open with a detailed investigation posted.
summaries.26336: Comments confirm the semantics-merge bug persists through stable 3.7.x. A workaround of wrapping `TextField` in `Semantics(explicitChildNodes: true)` prevents the merge but creates two accessibility announcements instead of one. A more refined workaround using `ExcludeSemantics` on the sibling text and `Semantics(label: ...)` on the field achieves a single announcement with working double-tap-to-edit. No PR mentioned; status is open.
summaries.142882: Comments confirm the bug is Android-only (iOS and web work correctly). Root cause identified in discussion: on Android with `enableSuggestions: false`, the underlying `InputType.TYPE_TEXT_VARIATION_VISIBLE_PASSWORD` flag causes the IME to handle backspace via a hardware-key path (`DeleteCharacterIntent`) rather than the `MethodChannel` text-update path, so a custom `TextInputClient` never receives a `updateEditingValue` for delete. The workaround is to intercept `DeleteCharacterIntent` via `FocusableActionDetector`. A separate but related bug (#143364) was filed for number-entry issues. Status: open, workaround documented.
summaries.79040: Comments confirm pressing Tab first then Shift (Tab+Shift order) moves focus forward and then back, while the intended Shift+Tab (Shift first) moves focus backward correctly. The root cause is that Flutter's shortcut system does not account for key-press order when evaluating modifier combinations. A team member noted this was a known gap and changes were being considered. The bug is reproducible on latest stable and master on web. Status: open, no PR.
summaries.159634: Root cause identified by a community member: `InputConnectionAdaptor.getCursorAnchorInfo()` in the Android engine never calls `setInsertionMarkerLocation`, so the IME candidate window always appears at the origin (lower-left corner). The commenter built a custom engine patch using `mLayout.getPrimaryHorizontal` and `mLayout.getLineTop/Bottom` for the horizontal coordinate, which corrected horizontal placement but left vertical at 0. The issue is in `shell/platform/android/io/flutter/plugins/editing/InputConnectionAdaptor.java`. Affects all Android devices when using a physical keyboard with CJK IME. Status: root cause confirmed, partial patch in community discussion, no official PR yet.
summaries.119502: Comments confirm the bug reproduces on stable and master when using `ExtendSelectionByCharacterIntent` on Android (emulator and physical devices). The Android IME sends back multiple `updateEditingValue` calls that collapse the selection after the client sets an uncollapsed one; this also occurs with the delta API. No fix or PR is mentioned. A follow-up comment noted the bug also affects Korean backspace with the delta model. Status: stalled, awaiting further investigation.
summaries.39755: Comments narrow the scope: the inaccurate selection highlight affects Korean, Arabic, Hebrew, and Persian scripts but not Cyrillic (Russian). Using a language-appropriate font (e.g., Amiri for Arabic) fixes the issue for pure RTL text, but mixed Latin+RTL text remains broken even with a proper font. The `selectionHeightStyle`/`selectionWidthStyle` options partially helped but did not fully resolve the issue. The problem is present in all text-alignment modes for RTL scripts, not only justified text. Status: open since 2019, no PR, long-standing stall.
summaries.142894: Comments confirm the wrong deletion range is reproducible on stable 3.16.x and master when typing Korean on a physical iOS device. The issue is specific to iOS (not Android or web): iOS sends a batch of deltas where the deletion range includes the invisible prefix characters (`". "`) used by super_editor to detect backspace-at-beginning. No fix or PR is mentioned in comments. Status: open, confirmed iOS-only, assigned to text-input team.
